Phase 19 PR91-PR95 (2026-05-10): Production identity and authorization boundary — Agentor:Auth mode configuration (Fake|Header|Jwt) with startup validation and safe defaults; configurable JWT actor-id/display-name/role claim mapping; authorization primitives (AgentorPermission, AuthorizationDecision, IAuthorizationDecisionService); role-based default authorization policy; endpoint permission enforcement for governance review, audit export, policy bundle read/write, and profile activation; security docs auth-boundary + deployment-threat-notes. acceptanceItems.description: Design doc defines ReviewCheckpoint, ResumeCursor, PendingPlanStep, ReviewedToolContinuation with FailFast/ContinueOnFailure/SkipRemaining interactions and all invariants.
Design doc states approval never overrides Deny.
Design doc states approval does not canonize knowledge.
PlanResumeCursor identifies plan step and pending tool.
Cursor cannot resume completed or failed terminal runs.
Tests cover invalid cursor transitions.
Approved review resumes from the pending step.
Remaining steps execute in order.
Tolerated failures (ContinueOnFailure) preserve existing semantics.
Deny cannot be resumed.
Approve resumes multi-step plan via API.
Reject fails reviewed run via API.
RequestChanges and Escalate record decision without executing tools via API.
Evaluation snapshot proves resumed plan completion (review-gated-multistep-plan.json).

acceptanceItems.description: Stale harness construction debris is removed and repo-clean flags it if it returns.
Resumed-step EscalateToReview leaves a pending reviewable tool call after deny/tool failure so human review can act safely.
Deferred-items manifest now lists only SCOPE-001 as active and records PR53-005 closure evidence.
Phase 13 alias endpoints enforce the same authorization checks as canonical governance routes.
Jwt role fallback is hardened: missing or unrecognized role claim no longer defaults to HumanOperator.
Review inbox alias endpoint is authorization-protected with read permission.
Security docs explicitly distinguish Jwt principal consumption from JwtBearer token validation middleware setup.
Durable run queue abstraction and record type introduced.
Queue enqueue persists durable queue item.
Worker can claim durable queue item.
Durable queue survives process restart.
RunQueueHostedService and RunWorkerOptions added with disabled-by-default behavior.
Worker processes durable queue items when enabled.
Worker respects execution leases and does not process contested work.
OutboxHostedService and OutboxDispatchOptions added with disabled-by-default behavior.
Hosted dispatcher sends pending outbox messages when enabled.
Retry and poison behavior is validated for hosted dispatch.
EfOutboxStore.TryMarkDispatchingAsync uses atomic conditional update.
Competing dispatchers cannot both acquire dispatch ownership.
Operational status endpoints added: GET /api/v1/ops/queue|outbox|leases.
Operational endpoints are read-only and avoid secret material.
Athanor HTTP adapter contract tests cover latest snapshot, canonical, evidence search, candidate submit, review queue, 404/null, and non-2xx error mapping.
No Canonize route on Athanor HTTP contract paths (port remains read/candidate/review only).
Conexus HTTP adapter maps model completion, telemetry fields, declared budget JSON pass-through, non-2xx errors, and client timeout to canceled/timeout exceptions (no provider SDKs).
Model gateway tool telemetry still aggregates into RunManifest via Application.
MCP HTTP adapter contract tests cover server list, tool discovery, invoke, invalid risk fallback, and HTTP error mapping.
MCP catalog descriptors remain outside Domain; tool registry MCP keys are deterministic.
External-agent HTTP client maps capabilities, invocation, non-canon flag from wire, and structured HTTP errors.
External invoke tool output carries non-canon markers from gateway result.
Policy deny or requires-review prevents external-agent protocol client invocation (no HTTP/fake invoke on blocked path).
Integration compatibility matrix documents Fake/Http/Disabled modes, required endpoints, and known limits.
Shared IntegrationHttpError helper throws status-code-bearing HttpRequestException for integration HTTP failures.
Upstream error bodies are truncated and best-effort redacted (Bearer, JSON apiKey/token/password/secret/authorization, key=value patterns).
Review inbox lists RequiresReview runs with pagination metadata and review reason without per-row full loads.
HTTP workflow produces RequiresReview via runtime policy, pending inbox shows run, approve clears inbox.
Run timeline exposes deterministic v2 groups for plan steps, skills, policy decisions, and review decisions.
Operator dashboard adds read-only queue/outbox/integration/policy-runtime/quality/deferred-risk modules backed by existing repositories or IIntegrationStatusReader.
Audit export/packet supports canonical, pretty, redaction report, and hash-only variants with identical canonical SHA-256.
Operator docs cover review workflow, run debugging, and audit export variants aligned with endpoints.
GET /api/v1/operator/dashboard requires OpsRead; Service forbidden; actor resolution failure yields unauthorized.
Security and operator docs state dashboard OpsRead requirement aligned with ops endpoints.
